results.possibleIdentity: Harold Hagen is an electrical engineer based in Seattle, Washington, who has been active in the fields of electrical engineering and computer technology since the late 1960s. He is an alumnus of the University of Washington, where he was part of the Electrical Engineering Class of 1968. Professionally, he is associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, a non-profit group founded to promote collaborations between the artistic and technical communities. His technical expertise spans vacuum tube circuitry and early computer technology. In addition to his engineering career, Hagen is a recognized expert and author in the field of vintage electronics restoration. He authored the technical guide 'Dr. Know-It-All's 2nd Seeburg Reference,' which details the operation and repair of Seeburg jukebox mechanisms. He has also provided technical consultations for antique radio and car radio repairs in the Seattle area and has been featured in hobbyist publications such as Old Cars Weekly and Antique Radio Classified.
